title: Add a deliberate fade-to-black for movie handoffs
summary: 0.4.27 adds a short black transition overlay for movie-to-movie handoffs so next, previous, and playlist-end advances fade down intentionally instead of showing a harsher abrupt dark gap between videos.
reasoning: The current movie transition already contains a dark moment, but it feels accidental. The smallest safe upgrade is a presentation-layer fade that rides on the existing runtime surface without reopening the deeper single-player media pipeline.

changes.summary: Adds a runtime transition-fade quad that darkens movie-to-movie handoffs and then releases once the next movie binding is ready.
changes.reasoning: This makes the handoff feel intentional while staying inside the proven screen-binding seam.

changes.summary: Hooks the fade release into both hosted and non-hosted screen refresh paths.
changes.reasoning: The same movie transition behavior should hold whether the player is running on the packaged screen CUA or another supported surface.

verification: Stamp version 0.4.27 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs, then build the player release in package-only mode.
Verify the packaged artifact is named FrameAngel.Player.0.4.27.var and contains the 0.4.27 asset, plugin, preset, and demo scene.
Verify the player plugin compiles after the new transition-fade seam is added.
knownIssues: This slice is a presentation-layer fade only; it does not change the underlying single-player video handoff model.
Live VaM proof is still needed to judge whether the 0.25 second fade feels right under real multi-instance load.
